narrative: Early evening, the light going long and gold through the remaining snow. Cole finally opens the front door and steps back and the dog comes in. Not quickly — slow, belly low, watching him — and goes straight under the kitchen table and lies down with a sound that Cole would call a sigh if he assigned human things to animals, which he doesn’t, but which he notices. Downtown, the new restaurant on Montana Avenue — a place called Sidetrack that’s trying to be casual and almost succeeding — had its soft opening tonight and it’s fuller than anyone expected. Petra and Marcus both came alone, got seated at the same two-top because there was nothing else, and are now having the kind of conversation that only happens between two people who didn’t plan to talk to each other: honest, unhurried, free of the performance that comes with intention. She tells him about the client review. He doesn’t tell her about the incident report but she senses something and doesn’t push. Billings is doing what it does — putting people in the same room and letting proximity do the work.
events: Cole brings the stray dog inside for the first time — it lies under the kitchen table and doesn’t move for two hours
A new restaurant opens downtown with a soft launch — Petra and Marcus both end up there independently and share a table because the place is full
